title: Gene Calling with Prodigal
author: James E Thornton Jr
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.kixcufn

steps.instruction: Login to the HPC and move into ocelote. $ ssh hpc$ ocelote $ ssh hpc $ ocelote
Move into your class directory. $ cd /rsgrps/bh_class/username Use YOUR username to be in the right directory

Copy the following into a new script called run-interactive.sh : #!/bin/bash#PBS -W group_list=bh_class#PBS -q windfall#PBS -l select=1:ncpus=2:mem=4gb#PBS -l walltime=24:00:00#PBS -l cput=24:00:00#PBS -M netid@email.arizona.edu#PBS -m bea Replace netid #!/bin/bash #PBS -W group_list=bh_class #PBS -q windfall #PBS -l select=1:ncpus=2:mem=4gb #PBS -l walltime=24:00:00 #PBS -l cput=24:00:00 #PBS -M netid@email.arizona.edu #PBS -m bea Replace netid
Submit run-interactive.sh interactively using qsub: qsub -I run-interactive.sh The capital
Once the job is ready move back into your class directory. $ cd /rsgrps/bh_class/username Use YOUR username
Make a directory named prodigal: $ mkdir prodigal

Move into your assembly directory which contains your contigs: Note: Its possible your contigs are in another directory (megahit-out). Move to the directory where final_contigs.fa are located. Remember final_contigs.fa is the combined assemblies from your partner. $ cd /rsgrps/bh_class/username/assembly

Load prodigal and run it on your final_contigs.fa to generate gene calls. Since final_contigs.fa may be located somewhere other than the assembly directory, you can write out the full paths to make sure the output goes where its suppose to: $ prodigal -i /path/to/final_contigs.fa -o /rsgrps/bh_class/username/prodigal/gene_calls -a /rsgrps/bh_class/username/prodigal/proteins.faa -d /rsgrps/bh_class/username/prodigal/nucleotides.fna The prodigal command is executed in one line. $ prodigal -i final_contigs.fa -o ../prodigal/gene_calls -a ../prodigal/proteins.faa -d ../prodigal/nucleotides.fna IMPORTANT: make sure to run prodigal on the final_contigs.fa file which was generated when combining assemblies with your partner.The output will be placed in the prodigal directory that was created and the file name will be gene_calls in addition to a file containing the protein (proteins.faa) and nucleotide (nucleotide.faa) sequences for the genes. $ prodigal -i final_contigs.fa -o ../prodigal/gene_calls -a ../prodigal/proteins.faa -d ../prodigal/nucleotides.fna IMPORTANT: make sure to run prodigal on the final_contigs.fa file which was generated when combining assemblies with your partner. The output will be placed in the prodigal directory that was created and the file name will be gene_calls in addition to a file containing the protein (proteins.faa) and nucleotide (nucleotide.faa) sequences for the genes.
Move into your prodigal directory and make sure the gene calls were generated. $ cd /rsgrps/bh_class/username/prodigal$ head gene_calls $ cd /rsgrps/bh_class/username/prodigal $ head gene_calls
Use your scripting or unix skills to detect how many genes were detected in your contigs file. Create a table that shows the number of 'ORFs' or open reading frames detected on the contigs.
